sections.paragraph:
ἔκδοτος, ον (s. ἐκδίδωμι; since Hdt. 3, 1, 1; Polyb.; Palaeph. 41, 2; Vett. Val.; PGrenf I, 1 [1], 7 [II b.c.]; Jos., Ant. 6, 316 al.; Sym.; Theod.) pert. to being handed over, given up, delivered up Ac 2:23 (of delivery to an enemy Hdt. 3, 1, 1; Aeschin. 3, 142). ἑαυτὸν ἔ. δέδωκα τῷ θανάτῳ I have delivered myself up to death ISm 4:2 (διδόναι τινὰ ἔ. as Demosth. 23, 85; Polyb. 20, 10, 5; 28, 4, 11; SIG 372, 13 [III b.c.]; Bel 22 Theod.; cp. Vett. Val. 106, 24; 220, 16).—DELG s.v. δίδωμι A2. M-M.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Palaeph Palaeph , date uncertain—List 5

PGrenf I PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 301
